title: GitHub treats a skipped required status check as satisfied
rule: GitHub branch protection treats a skipped required status check as satisfied, not as a failure — so a PR can merge even though that check never actually ran. It bites fan-in jobs ('all checks passed') built with `needs: [...]`: if an upstream job fails, the fan-in job is skipped by default, leaving the required check green by absence. Make such gate jobs fail-closed: add `if: always()` plus a step that fails unless every needed result is success (treat failure, cancelled and skipped as blocking). Then a failing gate reports a real failure and the merge is correctly blocked.
triggers: a failing PR merged even though a required check was skipped
fan-in gate job is skipped when an upstream job fails
branch protection treats a skipped check as passed
required status check skipped still allowed the merge
